_meta.note: Built to the Armagh-locked standard. Roscommon were the story of Connacht: a clean sweep of the province's minor, U20 and senior titles, the senior won by beating reigning champions Galway in the final after trailing by nine. Then eliminated from the All-Ireland series in two games, the worst finish by a provincial champion since the 16-team format began.
_meta.verification: All scorelines, venues and referees verified against primary match reports (gaa.ie, RTE, the42, Irish Times, HoganStand) and the official Connacht GAA and Roscommon county-board fixtures records. Full scorer lists and two-pointer counts sourced for all five championship games.

manager.name: Mark Dowd
manager.note: First season as Roscommon senior manager, a native of the county. Previously involved with Roscommon's 2006 All-Ireland minor-winning management. Made midfield a specific point of improvement during the championship. Oversaw a clean sweep of the county's senior, U20 and minor Connacht titles in 2026.

source.snippet: Roscommon manager Mark Dowd, who has made balance and composure his trademark since stepping into the role of manager of his native county.

league.note: Described in championship coverage as one of the two most-improved league sides of 2026 (with Mayo), finishing next in the pecking order behind the two 2025 All-Ireland finalists. Exact final table position and round-by-round results not yet sourced — flagged as a gap.

source.snippet: In the National League, Mayo and Roscommon were the two sides that showed the most progress, finishing next in the pecking order behind last year's two All-Ireland finalists.

Roscommon were Connacht champions but were knocked out in Round 2B, equivalent to 13th-16th place; this is the worst finish by a provincial champion since the 16-team All-Ireland series was introduced in 2023.

scorers.note: 1 free; withdrew after a heavy challenge

results.note: Routine win away to New York at Gaelic Park. Full scorer list not yet sourced; New York's own file will carry the opposing perspective.

source.snippet: Roscommon proved that their renaissance under new manager Mark Dowd is the real thing, as they produced a mercurial second half display... after scoring 1-3 in the opening half hour, Roscommon racked up an incredible 0-22 in the next 30 minutes of play.

results.note: Trailed 1-11 to 1-8 at half-time before scoring 1-17 to 0-7 in the second half to win by 10 — a statement win away to the pre-match favourites. Murtagh's 1-10 was the standout individual tally of the Connacht series. Mayo's two-pointer count (2: O'Donoghue, McDonald, both frees) read from the same report.

results.venue: King & Moffatt Dr. Hyde Park, Roscommon

scorers.note: two goals in little over a minute; man of the match contender
captain; 1 two-point free, 2 frees; late two-point free sealed it
goal off a parried Darragh Heneghan shot
substitute; two-pointer in the closing surge

source.snippet: The Roscommon revolution keeps on rolling... when two Roscommon men jumped for a kickout and the resultant breakaway saw Damien Comer hammer the ball into the roof of the net to make it 2-22 to 3-13 on 61 minutes, it looked like it was all over.

results.note: Roscommon's 25th Connacht title and first since 2019, completing a clean sweep of the province's minor, U20 and senior crowns. Their first home win over Galway at this stage since 1990, ending a 36-year wait. Trailed by eight in the first half and by nine (2-22 to 3-13) with nine minutes left after Damien Comer's goal, then closed the game with eight unanswered points; Galway's Shane Walsh dropped the late equaliser attempt a yard wide. Galway's two-pointer count (5: Walsh x2 incl 1 free, Conroy x2, Molloy x1) read from the same report. Referee: Sean Hurson. Attendance: 22,799.

results.venue: King & Moffatt Dr. Hyde Park, Roscommon

scorers.note: 5th-minute goal, his fifth of the championship
substitute; two-pointer that briefly forced a leveller

source.snippet: Tyrone their biggest win of the year so far, and derailing Roscommon's incredible momentum... Ethan Jordan to fire over the lead score with 35 seconds left.

results.note: Led 2-15 to 2-13 late before a Mattie Donnelly goal turned it; Paul Carey's two-pointer levelled it at 69 minutes, but Tyrone won the kickout and Ethan Jordan kicked the winning free with 35 seconds left. Tyrone's two-pointer count (1: E. Jordan) read from the same report. Referee: David Gough. Attendance: ~16,181.

source.snippet: Monaghan suffocated the life out of Roscommon with a second-half performance... built around Rory Beggan's immaculate kickouts and Conor McCarthy's quarter-backing of the winning side.

results.note: Elimination game, played without the influential Enda Smith (ruled out beforehand). Roscommon couldn't find their provincial-campaign attacking verve and were outscored 0-11 to 0-7 after half-time. This 13th-16th-place finish is the worst by any provincial champion since the 16-team format began in 2023. Monaghan's two-pointer count (2: McCarron x2 from the wider Monaghan file) noted; the single Roscommon two-pointer is Murtagh's. Referee: Paul Faloon (Down).

collection_meta.collected_by: Claude (research session, not yet hand-verified by Rory)
fields_not_found: Allianz NFL Division 1 exact final table position (know only that they were among the two most-improved sides, finishing behind the 2025 finalists)
Full scorer list for the New York Connacht quarter-final (have only the scoreline)
Attendance figures for games other than the Connacht final and Tyrone R1
collection_meta.notes: Roscommon is one of the strongest season-narrative candidates in the whole book: a provincial clean sweep, a final won from nine points down against the reigning champions in front of a euphoric home crowd, then the steepest possible fall — out of the All-Ireland series in two games, the worst provincial-champion finish of the new era. Darragh Heneghan (3-5 across the Galway and Tyrone games) and captain Diarmuid Murtagh (1-10 v Mayo) are the names to carry into any narrative or signature-panel work. The Connacht final is a clear Tier 1 / signature-moment candidate.
